name: discid

metadata.name: discid
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU Lesser General Public License v3 or later (LGPLv3+)
Topic :: Multimedia :: Sound/Audio :: CD Audio :: CD Ripping
Topic :: Software Development :: Libraries :: Python Modules

documentation:
# Libdiscid Python bindings

Python-discid implements Python bindings for MusicBrainz Libdiscid. This
module works with Python 2 >= 2.6, or Python 3 >= 3.1.
Libdiscid >= 0.2.2 is needed.

Libdiscid's main purpose is the calculation of an identifier of audio
discs (disc ID) to use for the [MusicBrainz database](http://musicbrainz.org).

That identifier is calculated from the TOC of the disc, similar to the
freeDB CDDB identifier. Libdiscid can calculate MusicBrainz disc IDs and
freeDB disc IDs.
Additionally the MCN of the disc and ISRCs from the tracks can be extracted.

This module is a close binding that offloads all relevant data
storage and calculation to Libdiscid. On the other hand it gives a
pythonic API and uses objects and exceptions.

The official API documentation can be found at [readthedocs.org]

For more information on Libdiscid see [libdiscid].

For more information about the calculation of these disc ids see [Disc
ID Calculation][disc id calculation].

## Usage

```python
# this will load Libdiscid
import discid

disc = discid.read()  # use default device
print "id: %s" % disc.id  # Python 2
print("id: %s" % disc.id) # Python 3
```

See also the examples.py.

## License

This module is released under the GNU Lesser General Public License
Version 3. See COPYING.LESSER for details.

## Bugs

You can submit tickets at [GitHub].

[disc id calculation]: http://musicbrainz.org/doc/Disc_ID_Calculation
[github]: https://github.com/JonnyJD/python-discid
[libdiscid]: http://musicbrainz.org/doc/libdiscid
[readthedocs.org]: https://python-discid.readthedocs.org/

modules.name: discid
documentation:
Python binding of Libdiscid

Libdiscid is a library to calculate MusicBrainz Disc IDs.
This module provides a python-like API for that functionality.

The user is expected to create a {class}`Disc` object
using {func}`read` or {func}`put` and extract the generated information.

Importing this module will open libdiscid at the same time
and will raise {exc}`OSError` when libdiscid is not found.

variables.name: discid.LIBDISCID_VERSION_STRING
documentation:
The version string of the loaded libdiscid in the form `libdiscid x.y.z`.
For old versions the string is `libdiscid < 0.4.0`.

variables.name: discid.FEATURES
documentation:
The features libdiscid supports for the platform as a list of strings.
Some Functions can raise {exc}`NotImplementedError` when a feature
is not available.
Some features might not be implemented in this python module,
see {data}`FEATURES_IMPLEMENTED`.

variables.name: discid.FEATURES_IMPLEMENTED
documentation:
The features implemented in this python module as a list of strings.
Some might not be available for your platform, see {data}`FEATURES`.

functions.name: discid.disc.read
documentation:
Reads the TOC from the device given as string
and returns a {class}`Disc` object.

That string can be either of:
{obj}`str <python:str>`, {obj}`unicode` or {obj}`bytes`.
However, it should in no case contain non-ASCII characters.
If no device is given, a default, also given by {func}`get_default_device`
is used.

You can optionally add a subset of the features in
{data}`FEATURES` or the whole list to read more than just the TOC.
In contrast to libdiscid, {func}`read` won't read any
of the additional features by default.

A {exc}`DiscError` exception is raised when the reading fails,
and {exc}`NotImplementedError` when libdiscid doesn't support
reading discs on the current platform.

functions.name: discid.disc.put
documentation:
Creates a TOC based on the information given
and returns a {class}`Disc` object.

Takes the `first` track and `last` **audio** track as {obj}`int`.
`disc_sectors` is the end of the last audio track,
normally the total sector count of the disc.
`track_offsets` is a list of all audio track offsets.

Depending on how you get the total sector count,
you might have to substract 11400 (2:32 min.) for discs with data tracks.

A {exc}`TOCError` exception is raised when illegal parameters
are provided.

:::{seealso}
{musicbrainz}`Disc ID Calculation`
:::

classes.name: discid.disc.DiscError
documentation: {func}`read` will raise this exception when an error occured.

classes.name: discid.disc.TOCError
documentation:
{func}`put` will raise this exception when illegal paramaters
are provided.

documentation:
The initialization will reserve some memory
for internal data structures.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._get_error_msg
documentation: Get the error message for the last error with the object.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.read
documentation:
Reads the TOC from the device given as string

The user is supposed to use {func}`discid.read`.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.put
documentation:
Creates a TOC based on the input given.

The user is supposed to use {func}`discid.put`.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._get_id
documentation: Gets the current MusicBrainz disc ID

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._get_freedb_id
documentation: Gets the current FreeDB disc ID

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._get_submission_url
documentation:
Give an URL to submit the current TOC
as a new Disc ID to MusicBrainz.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._get_toc_string
documentation:
The TOC suitable as value of the `toc parameter`
when accessing the MusicBrainz Web Service.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._get_first_track_num
documentation: Gets the first track number

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._get_last_track_num
documentation: Gets the last track number

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._get_sectors
documentation: Gets the total number of sectors on the disc

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._get_mcn
documentation: Gets the current Media Catalogue Number (MCN/UPC/EAN)

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.id
documentation:
This is the MusicBrainz {musicbrainz}`Disc ID`,
a {obj}`unicode` or {obj}`str <python:str>` object.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.freedb_id
documentation:
This is the {musicbrainz}`FreeDB` Disc ID (without category),
a {obj}`unicode` or {obj}`str <python:str>` object.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.submission_url
documentation:
Disc ID / TOC Submission URL for MusicBrainz

With this url you can submit the current TOC
as a new MusicBrainz {musicbrainz}`Disc ID`.
This is a {obj}`unicode` or {obj}`str <python:str>` object.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.toc_string
documentation:
The TOC suitable as value of the `toc parameter`
when accessing the MusicBrainz Web Service.

This is a {obj}`unicode` or {obj}`str <python:str>` object
and enables fuzzy searching when the actual Disc ID is not found.

Note that this is the unencoded value, which still contains spaces.

:::{seealso}
[MusicBrainz Web Service](http://musicbrainz.org/doc/Development/XML_Web_Service/Version_2#discid)
:::

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.first_track_num
documentation: Number of the first track

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.last_track_num
documentation: Number of the last **audio** track

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.sectors
documentation: Total length in sectors

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.seconds
documentation: Total length in seconds

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.mcn
documentation:
This is the Media Catalogue Number (MCN/UPC/EAN)

It is set after the `"mcn"` feature was requested on a read
and supported by the platform or {obj}`None`.
If set, this is a {obj}`unicode` or {obj}`str <python:str>` object.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.tracks
documentation: A list of {class}`Track` objects for this Disc.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc._free
documentation: This will free the internal allocated memory for the object.

methods.name: discid.disc.Disc.__exit__
documentation: deprecated {keyword}`with` usage
